# Build a birthday book web application
_Exported on 12/15/2025 at 09:37:39 GMT+8 from Cursor (2.2.20)_

---

**User**

Create a mobile-responsive web application using Google Material Design guidelines of an illustrated book for my son Kai's 1st birthday. Use the writing style, illustration style, book structure and format as defined in the attached file children-board-book-style-guide.md. While the children-board-book-style-guide.md file has instructions on script generation, I have already created a script for this specific book in the attached file script-for-kai-1st-birthday.md, so adhere to the script and instructions for each page in the book as defined in this file.

The web page should support 2 pages:
1. A page that shows a library of books. For now, there will be one book in the library, Kai’s First Birthday Book. A user can select a book in the library to view.
2. A page to show a single book at a time that has been chosen from the library. The resulting book needs to simulate the experience of flipping through a physical book, using left and right arrows to flip pages, with life-like animation for page turning. The first page will show the front cover page only, the last page will show the back cover page only, and every other page flip will show 2 pages - left page and right - just like flipping through a real physical book. The application needs to support the exporting of the book to a PDF file, so there should be an export button with PDF as the only option for now. There also needs to be a button for the user to select to go back to the book library page.

In addition to script-for-kai-1st-birthday.md, I have curated 2 collections of images in the project folder.
1. “/images/people/“ - This contains photos of Kai and his parents Dian and Tony. Use the images in this folder of Kai as inspiration for the drawings of Kai throughout the book. Use the images in this folder of Dian and Tony for inspiration for drawing their characters on the 
2. “/images/storyline/“ - This contains photos of various scenes and objects, some with Kai and some without, to be used as inspiration for the illustrations created throughout the book. The image title defines the scene or object that the photo depicts, so use that information to figure out which scene in the book to use each photo as inspiration for creating those illustrations.

Use Gemini Flash 2.5 to create the illustration images for each page. Tell me what I need to do to enable this. Do NOT create text (the book script) directly within the images, that should be overlaid on top of the generated images. So when generating images using Gemini Flash 2.5, do NOT give it the script text, just the illustration instructions. Tell Gemini Flash 2.5 to adhere to the illustration guidelines in the children-board-book-style-guide.md file.

Ask me clarifying questions until you are 95% confident in your ability to create this application according to my expectations. Ask each question one at a time so I don’t get overwhelmed.@script-for-kai-1st-birthday.md @children-board-book-style-guide.md  @people @storyline 

---

**Cursor**

Reviewing the script and style guide, then asking clarifying questions.


**Question 1: Gemini Flash 2.5 API integration**

For generating illustrations with Gemini Flash 2.5, how should I handle the API key?

- Option A: Store it in an environment variable (e.g., `.env`) and read it at runtime
- Option B: You’ll provide it when I need to generate images
- Option C: Use a different method

Also, should the app generate images on demand when viewing the book, or should I generate them once and save them locally?

---

**User**

A

---

**Cursor**

**Question 2: Image generation timing**

When should the illustrations be generated?

- Option A: Generate all 18 pages upfront (before the app runs) and save them locally
- Option B: Generate on demand when viewing the book (cache after first generation)
- Option C: Generate once during initial setup/build, then reuse saved images

Which do you prefer?

---

**User**

C

---

**Cursor**

**Question 3: Generated images storage location**

Where should the generated illustrations be saved?

- Option A: Create a new folder like `images/kai-book/` or `images/generated/` with numbered files (e.g., `page-01.png`, `page-02.png`)
- Option B: Save them in a different location (please specify)
- Option C: Use a different naming/structure convention (please specify)

Which do you prefer?

---

**User**

/books/kais-first-birthday-book

---

**Cursor**

**Question 4: PDF export format**

For PDF export, how should the pages be arranged?

- Option A: Each page as a separate PDF page (18 pages total: front cover, title page, 15 content pages, back cover)
- Option B: Two-page spreads where applicable (e.g., pages 4-5 together, pages 8-9 together), single pages otherwise
- Option C: All pages as two-page spreads (front cover alone, then pairs, back cover alone)

Which format should the PDF use?

---

**User**

C

---

**Cursor**

**Question 5: Page turning animation**

For the page-turning animation, what level of realism do you want?

- Option A: Simple CSS flip animation (quick, lightweight)
- Option B: Realistic 3D page curl with shadow effects (more complex, heavier)
- Option C: A library like Turn.js or similar (balanced)

Which do you prefer?

---

**User**

C

---

**Cursor**

**Question 6: Text overlay implementation**

For text overlaid on illustrations, how should it be handled?

- Option A: HTML/CSS text positioned absolutely over the images (editable, accessible)
- Option B: Render text directly onto the images during PDF generation (text becomes part of the image)
- Option C: Both — HTML/CSS for the web viewer, rendered onto images for PDF export

Which approach do you prefer?

---

**User**

C

---

**Cursor**

**Question 7: Technology stack**

Which framework/library should I use for the web app?

- Option A: React with Material-UI (MUI)
- Option B: Vue.js with Vuetify
- Option C: Vanilla JavaScript with Material Design Components (MDC)
- Option D: Another framework/library (please specify)

Which do you prefer?

---

**User**

What would you suggest? Can you give me a short pro and con for each approach?

---

**Cursor**

**Option A: React with Material-UI (MUI)**
- Pros: Strong ecosystem, component reusability, good for scaling, MUI is mature
- Cons: Requires build tooling, more overhead for a simple app, steeper learning curve

**Option B: Vue.js with Vuetify**
- Pros: Simpler than React, Vuetify is solid, good performance
- Cons: Still needs build tooling, smaller ecosystem than React

**Option C: Vanilla JavaScript with Material Design Components (MDC)**
- Pros: No build step, lightweight, fast, easy to deploy, works well with Turn.js and PDF libraries
- Cons: More manual DOM work, less component reusability

**Recommendation: Option C (Vanilla JS with MDC)**

Reasons:
- Simple app (library page + book viewer)
- Turn.js works well with vanilla JS
- PDF generation is straightforward
- Faster development without build setup
- Easy to host/deploy

If you plan to add many features or multiple books later, React might be better. For this scope, vanilla JS is a good fit.

Should I proceed with Option C?

---

**User**

Yes, C

---

Creating the initial todo list and starting implementation.


Creating the project structure and core files:


```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Baby Got Book - Library</title>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="styles/main.css">
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Roboto:wght@300;400;500;700&display=swap">
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/icon?family=Material+Icons">
</head>
<body>
    <div class="mdc-layout">
        <header class="mdc-top-app-bar">
            <div class="mdc-top-app-bar__row">
                <section class="mdc-top-app-bar__section mdc-top-app-bar__section--align-start">
                    <span class="mdc-top-app-bar__title">Baby Got Book</span>
                </section>
            </div>
        </header>

        <main class="mdc-top-app-bar--fixed-adjust">
            <div class="library-container">
                <h1 class="library-title">My Book Library</h1>
                <div class="book-grid" id="bookGrid">
                    <!-- Books will be dynamically loaded here -->
                </div>
            </div>
        </main>
    </div>

    <script src="js/library.js"></script>
</body>
</html>

```

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Kai's First Birthday Book</title>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="styles/main.css">
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="styles/book-viewer.css">
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Roboto:wght@300;400;500;700&display=swap">
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/icon?family=Material+Icons">
    <script src="https://code.jquery.com/jquery-3.6.0.min.js"></script>
    <script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/turn.js/4.1.0/turn.min.js"></script>
</head>
<body>
    <div class="book-viewer-container">
        <header class="mdc-top-app-bar book-viewer-header">
            <div class="mdc-top-app-bar__row">
                <section class="mdc-top-app-bar__section mdc-top-app-bar__section--align-start">
                    <button class="mdc-icon-button material-icons" id="backButton" aria-label="Back to library">
                        arrow_back
                    </button>
                    <span class="mdc-top-app-bar__title" id="bookTitle">Kai's First Birthday Book</span>
                </section>
                <section class="mdc-top-app-bar__section mdc-top-app-bar__section--align-end">
                    <button class="mdc-button mdc-button--raised" id="exportPdfButton">
                        <span class="mdc-button__ripple"></span>
                        <span class="mdc-button__label">Export PDF</span>
                    </button>
                </section>
            </div>
        </header>

        <main class="book-viewer-main">
            <div class="book-wrapper">
                <div class="book-controls book-controls-left">
                    <button class="mdc-icon-button material-icons page-turn-btn" id="prevPage" aria-label="Previous page">
                        chevron_left
                    </button>
                </div>
                
                <div id="book" class="book">
                    <!-- Pages will be dynamically generated here -->
                </div>

                <div class="book-controls book-controls-right">
                    <button class="mdc-icon-button material-icons page-turn-btn" id="nextPage" aria-label="Next page">
                        chevron_right
                    </button>
                </div>
            </div>

            <div class="page-indicator">
                <span id="currentPage">1</span> / <span id="totalPages">18</span>
            </div>
        </main>
    </div>

    <script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/jspdf/2.5.1/jspdf.umd.min.js"></script>
    <script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/html2canvas/1.4.1/html2canvas.min.js"></script>
    <script src="js/book-data.js"></script>
    <script src="js/book-viewer.js"></script>
</body>
</html>

```

```css
/* Material Design Color Palette */
:root {
    --mdc-theme-primary: #6200ee;
    --mdc-theme-primary-variant: #3700b3;
    --mdc-theme-secondary: #03dac6;
    --mdc-theme-background: #ffffff;
    --mdc-theme-surface: #ffffff;
    --mdc-theme-error: #b00020;
    --mdc-theme-on-primary: #ffffff;
    --mdc-theme-on-secondary: #000000;
    --mdc-theme-on-surface: #000000;
    --mdc-theme-on-error: #ffffff;
    
    --mdc-elevation-shadow-1: 0px 2px 1px -1px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.2), 0px 1px 1px 0px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.14), 0px 1px 3px 0px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.12);
    --mdc-elevation-shadow-2: 0px 3px 1px -2px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.2), 0px 2px 2px 0px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.14), 0px 1px 5px 0px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.12);
    --mdc-elevation-shadow-4: 0px 2px 4px -1px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.2), 0px 4px 5px 0px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.14), 0px 1px 10px 0px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.12);
    --mdc-elevation-shadow-8: 0px 5px 5px -3px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.2), 0px 8px 10px 1px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.14), 0px 3px 14px 2px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.12);
}

* {
    margin: 0;
    padding: 0;
    box-sizing: border-box;
}

body {
    font-family: 'Roboto', sans-serif;
    background-color: #f5f5f5;
    color: var(--mdc-theme-on-surface);
    line-height: 1.5;
}

/* Top App Bar */
.mdc-top-app-bar {
    background-color: var(--mdc-theme-primary);
    color: var(--mdc-theme-on-primary);
    position: fixed;
    top: 0;
    left: 0;
    right: 0;
    z-index: 7;
    box-shadow: var(--mdc-elevation-shadow-4);
}

.mdc-top-app-bar__row {
    display: flex;
    position: relative;
    box-sizing: border-box;
    width: 100%;
    height: 64px;
}

.mdc-top-app-bar__section {
    display: inline-flex;
    flex: 1 1 auto;
    align-items: center;
    min-width: 0;
    padding: 8px 12px;
    z-index: 1;
}

.mdc-top-app-bar__section--align-start {
    justify-content: flex-start;
    order: -1;
}

.mdc-top-app-bar__section--align-end {
    justify-content: flex-end;
    order: 1;
}

.mdc-top-app-bar__title {
    font-family: 'Roboto', sans-serif;
    font-size: 1.25rem;
    font-weight: 500;
    line-height: 2rem;
    letter-spacing: 0.0125em;
    text-decoration: inherit;
    text-transform: inherit;
    padding-left: 20px;
    padding-right: 0;
    white-space: nowrap;
    overflow: hidden;
    text-overflow: ellipsis;
}

.mdc-top-app-bar--fixed-adjust {
    padding-top: 64px;
}

/* Icon Button */
.mdc-icon-button {
    width: 48px;
    height: 48px;
    padding: 12px;
    font-size: 24px;
    display: inline-flex;
    align-items: center;
    justify-content: center;
    border: none;
    background: transparent;
    color: inherit;
    cursor: pointer;
    border-radius: 50%;
    transition: background-color 0.2s;
}

.mdc-icon-button:hover {
    background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1);
}

.mdc-icon-button:focus {
    outline: none;
    background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1);
}

.mdc-icon-button:active {
    background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.2);
}

/* Button */
.mdc-button {
    font-family: 'Roboto', sans-serif;
    font-size: 0.875rem;
    font-weight: 500;
    letter-spacing: 0.0892857143em;
    text-decoration: none;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    padding: 0 16px;
    height: 36px;
    border: none;
    border-radius: 4px;
    display: inline-flex;
    align-items: center;
    justify-content: center;
    cursor: pointer;
    position: relative;
    overflow: hidden;
    background-color: transparent;
    color: var(--mdc-theme-primary);
    transition: background-color 0.2s, box-shadow 0.2s;
}

.mdc-button--raised {
    background-color: var(--mdc-theme-primary);
    color: var(--mdc-theme-on-primary);
    box-shadow: var(--mdc-elevation-shadow-2);
}

.mdc-button--raised:hover {
    box-shadow: var(--mdc-elevation-shadow-4);
}

.mdc-button--raised:active {
    box-shadow: var(--mdc-elevation-shadow-8);
}

.mdc-button__ripple {
    position: absolute;
    top: 0;
    left: 0;
    right: 0;
    bottom: 0;
    overflow: hidden;
}

.mdc-button__label {
    position: relative;
    z-index: 1;
}

/* Library Page Styles */
.library-container {
    max-width: 1200px;
    margin: 0 auto;
    padding: 24px 16px;
}

.library-title {
    font-size: 2rem;
    font-weight: 400;
    margin-bottom: 32px;
    color: var(--mdc-theme-on-surface);
}

.book-grid {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(280px, 1fr));
    gap: 24px;
    padding-bottom: 32px;
}

.book-card {
    background-color: var(--mdc-theme-surface);
    border-radius: 4px;
    box-shadow: var(--mdc-elevation-shadow-2);
    overflow: hidden;
    cursor: pointer;
    transition: box-shadow 0.2s, transform 0.2s;
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
}

.book-card:hover {
    box-shadow: var(--mdc-elevation-shadow-8);
    transform: translateY(-4px);
}

.book-card-image {
    width: 100%;
    height: 320px;
    object-fit: cover;
    background-color: #e0e0e0;
}

.book-card-content {
    padding: 16px;
}

.book-card-title {
    font-size: 1.25rem;
    font-weight: 500;
    margin-bottom: 8px;
    color: var(--mdc-theme-on-surface);
}

.book-card-subtitle {
    font-size: 0.875rem;
    color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.6);
}

/* Responsive Design */
@media (max-width: 600px) {
    .book-grid {
        grid-template-columns: 1fr;
    }
    
    .library-title {
        font-size: 1.5rem;
    }
    
    .mdc-top-app-bar__title {
        font-size: 1rem;
        padding-left: 8px;
    }
}

```

```css
.book-viewer-container {
    min-height: 100vh;
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    background-color: #f5f5f5;
}

.book-viewer-header {
    position: fixed;
    top: 0;
    left: 0;
    right: 0;
    z-index: 10;
}

.book-viewer-main {
    flex: 1;
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    align-items: center;
    justify-content: center;
    padding: 80px 16px 32px;
    min-height: 100vh;
}

.book-wrapper {
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
    justify-content: center;
    gap: 16px;
    width: 100%;
    max-width: 1200px;
    margin-bottom: 24px;
}

.book-controls {
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
}

.page-turn-btn {
    background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9);
    color: var(--mdc-theme-primary);
    box-shadow: var(--mdc-elevation-shadow-2);
}

.page-turn-btn:hover {
    background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 1);
    box-shadow: var(--mdc-elevation-shadow-4);
}

.page-turn-btn:disabled {
    opacity: 0.5;
    cursor: not-allowed;
}

/* Turn.js Book Styles */
.book {
    width: 90vw;
    max-width: 800px;
    height: 600px;
    margin: 0 auto;
    box-shadow: var(--mdc-elevation-shadow-8);
}

.book .page {
    width: 50%;
    height: 100%;
    background-color: white;
    background-size: 100% 100%;
    background-repeat: no-repeat;
    background-position: center;
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
    justify-content: center;
    position: relative;
}

.book .page-content {
    width: 100%;
    height: 100%;
    position: relative;
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    align-items: center;
    justify-content: center;
    padding: 32px;
}

.book .page-image {
    width: 100%;
    height: 100%;
    object-fit: cover;
    position: absolute;
    top: 0;
    left: 0;
    z-index: 0;
}

.book .page-text {
    position: relative;
    z-index: 1;
    text-align: center;
    font-family: 'Roboto', sans-serif;
    color: #000;
    width: 100%;
    padding: 24px;
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    align-items: center;
    justify-content: center;
    min-height: 100%;
}

.book .page-text.white-text {
    color: #fff;
    text-shadow: 1px 1px 2px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
}

.book .page-text h1 {
    font-size: 2.5rem;
    font-weight: 700;
    margin-bottom: 16px;
    line-height: 1.2;
}

.book .page-text h2 {
    font-size: 2rem;
    font-weight: 500;
    margin-bottom: 12px;
    line-height: 1.3;
}

.book .page-text p {
    font-size: 1.5rem;
    line-height: 1.6;
    margin-bottom: 12px;
}

.book .page-text .large-text {
    font-size: 3rem;
    font-weight: 700;
}

.book .page-text .medium-text {
    font-size: 2rem;
    font-weight: 500;
}

.book .page-text .small-text {
    font-size: 1.25rem;
    font-weight: 400;
}

.book .page-text .emphasis {
    font-size: 2.5rem;
    font-weight: 700;
    text-transform: uppercase;
}

/* Page Indicator */
.page-indicator {
    font-size: 1rem;
    color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.6);
    margin-top: 16px;
    font-weight: 500;
}

/* Responsive Design for Book Viewer */
@media (max-width: 768px) {
    .book-wrapper {
        flex-direction: column;
        gap: 8px;
    }
    
    .book-controls {
        order: 2;
    }
    
    .book-controls-left {
        order: 1;
    }
    
    .book-controls-right {
        order: 3;
    }
    
    .book {
        width: 100vw;
        height: 70vh;
        max-height: 500px;
    }
    
    .book .page-text h1 {
        font-size: 1.75rem;
    }
    
    .book .page-text h2 {
        font-size: 1.5rem;
    }
    
    .book .page-text p {
        font-size: 1.25rem;
    }
    
    .book .page-text .large-text {
        font-size: 2rem;
    }
    
    .book .page-text .medium-text {
        font-size: 1.5rem;
    }
    
    .book .page-text .small-text {
        font-size: 1rem;
    }
    
    .book .page-text .emphasis {
        font-size: 1.75rem;
    }
    
    .book .page-content {
        padding: 16px;
    }
}

@media (max-width: 480px) {
    .book {
        height: 60vh;
        max-height: 400px;
    }
    
    .book .page-text {
        padding: 16px;
    }
    
    .book .page-text h1 {
        font-size: 1.5rem;
    }
    
    .book .page-text p {
        font-size: 1rem;
    }
}

```

Creating the JavaScript files:


```javascript
// Book data structure for Kai's First Birthday Book
const kaiBookData = {
    id: 'kais-first-birthday-book',
    title: "Kai's First Birthday Book",
    coverImage: 'images/books/kais-first-birthday-book/page-01.png',
    pages: [
        {
            pageNumber: 1,
            type: 'cover',
            text: {
                lines: [
                    { text: 'Yippee Kai Yeh', size: 'large', weight: 'bold' },
                    { text: 'turns 1', size: 'medium', weight: 'normal' }
                ],
                color: 'black'
            },
            image: 'images/books/kais-first-birthday-book/page-01.png',
            backgroundColor: '#87CEEB' // Soft sky blue
        },
        {
            pageNumber: 2,
            type: 'title',
            text: {
                lines: [
                    { text: 'Dear Kai,', size: 'medium', weight: 'normal' },
                    { text: 'Happy 1st birthday Kai, our little love.', size: 'small', weight: 'normal' },
                    { text: 'You fill our hearts with joy and wonder every day.', size: 'small', weight: 'normal' },
                    { text: 'Dream big and grand; your reach has no bounds.', size: 'small', weight: 'normal' },
                    { text: 'We are here to love and protect, and so excited for what\'s ahead.', size: 'small', weight: 'normal' },
                    { text: 'Love,', size: 'small', weight: 'normal' },
                    { text: 'Mommy and Daddy', size: 'medium', weight: 'normal' }
                ],
                color: 'black'
            },
            image: 'images/books/kais-first-birthday-book/page-02.png',
            backgroundColor: '#FFBD9A' // Warm peach
        },
        {
            pageNumber: 3,
            type: 'content',
            text: {
                lines: [
                    { text: 'Kai\'s a boy with a giggly grin,', size: 'medium', weight: 'normal' },
                    { text: 'Guess who\'s ONE? Let the fun begin!', size: 'medium', weight: 'bold' }
                ],
                color: 'black'
            },
            image: 'images/books/kais-first-birthday-book/page-03.png',
            backgroundColor: '#FFD966' // Bright pastel yellow
        },
        {
            pageNumber: 4,
            type: 'spread-left',
            text: {
                lines: [

```

```javascript
// Library page functionality
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', () => {
    const bookGrid = document.getElementById('bookGrid');
    
    // Load books from library
    bookLibrary.forEach(book => {
        const bookCard = createBookCard(book);
        bookGrid.appendChild(bookCard);
    });
});

function createBookCard(book) {
    const card = document.createElement('div');
    card.className = 'book-card';
    card.addEventListener('click', () => {
        window.location.href = `book-viewer.html?book=${book.id}`;
    });
    
    const image = document.createElement('img');
    image.className = 'book-card-image';
    image.src = book.coverImage;
    image.alt = book.title;
    image.onerror = function() {
        // Fallback if image doesn't exist yet
        this.style.backgroundColor = '#e0e0e0';
        this.style.display = 'flex';
        this.style.alignItems = 'center';
        this.style.justifyContent = 'center';
        this.alt = 'Cover image coming soon';
    };
    
    const content = document.createElement('div');
    content.className = 'book-card-content';
    
    const title = document.createElement('h2');
    title.className = 'book-card-title';
    title.textContent = book.title;
    
    const subtitle = document.createElement('p');
    subtitle.className = 'book-card-subtitle';
    subtitle.textContent = `${book.pages.length} pages`;
    
    content.appendChild(title);
    content.appendChild(subtitle);
    
    card.appendChild(image);
    card.appendChild(content);
    
    return card;
}

```

```javascript
// Book viewer functionality with Turn.js
let currentBook = null;
let bookInstance = null;

document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', () => {
    // Get book ID from URL
    const urlParams = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);
    const bookId = urlParams.get('book') || 'kais-first-birthday-book';
    
    // Find book in library
    currentBook = bookLibrary.find(b => b.id === bookId);
    
    if (!currentBook) {
        console.error('Book not found');
        return;
    }
    
    // Set book title
    document.getElementById('bookTitle').textContent = currentBook.title;
    document.getElementById('totalPages').textContent = currentBook.pages.length;
    
    // Initialize book
    initializeBook();
    
    // Set up event listeners
    setupEventListeners();
});

function initializeBook() {
    const bookElement = document.getElementById('book');
    bookElement.innerHTML = '';
    
    // Create pages based on book data
    currentBook.pages.forEach((page, index) => {
        const pageElement = createPageElement(page, index);
        bookElement.appendChild(pageElement);
    });
    
    // Initialize Turn.js
    bookInstance = $('#book').turn({
        width: 800,
        height: 600,
        autoCenter: true,
        pages: currentBook.pages.length,
        gradients: true,
        elevation: 50,
        when: {
            turning: function(event, page, view) {
                updatePageIndicator(page);
            }
        }
    });
    
    // Update page indicator for initial page
    updatePageIndicator(1);
    
    // Update navigation buttons
    updateNavigationButtons();
}

function createPageElement(page, index) {
    const pageDiv = document.createElement('div');
    pageDiv.className = 'page';
    
    const pageContent = document.createElement('div');
    pageContent.className = 'page-content';
    
    // Add background image
    const pageImage = document.createElement('img');
    pageImage.className = 'page-image';
    pageImage.src = page.image;
    pageImage.alt = `Page ${page.pageNumber}`;
    pageImage.onerror = function() {
        // Fallback background color if image doesn't exist
        this.style.display = 'none';
        pageContent.style.backgroundColor = page.backgroundColor || '#ffffff';
    };
    
    // Add text overlay
    const textDiv = document.createElement('div');
    textDiv.className = `page-text ${page.text.color === 'white' ? 'white-text' : ''}`};
    
    page.text.lines.forEach(line => {
        const lineElement = document.createElement('p');
        lineElement.className = `${line.size}-text`;
        lineElement.style.fontWeight = line.weight || 'normal';
        if (line.weight === 'bold') {
            lineElement.style.textTransform = 'uppercase';
        }
        lineElement.textContent = line.text;
        textDiv.appendChild(lineElement);
    });
    
    pageContent.appendChild(pageImage);
    pageContent.appendChild(textDiv);
    pageDiv.appendChild(pageContent);
    
    return pageDiv;
}

function setupEventListeners() {
    // Back button
    document.getElementById('backButton').addEventListener('click', () => {
        window.location.href = 'index.html';
    });
    
    // Previous page button
    document.getElementById('prevPage').addEventListener('click', () => {
        if (bookInstance) {
            bookInstance.turn('previous');
        }
    });
    
    // Next page button
    document.getElementById('nextPage').addEventListener('click', () => {
        if (bookInstance) {
            bookInstance.turn('next');
        }
    });
    
    // Keyboard navigation
    document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {
        if (e.key === 'ArrowLeft') {
            if (bookInstance) {
                bookInstance.turn('previous');
            }
        } else if (e.key === 'ArrowRight') {
            if (bookInstance) {
                bookInstance.turn('next');
            }
        }
    });
    
    // PDF export button
    document.getElementById('exportPdfButton').addEventListener('click', () => {
        exportToPDF();
    });
    
    // Update navigation buttons when page changes
    $('#book').bind('turning', function(event, page, view) {
        updateNavigationButtons();
    });
}

function updatePageIndicator(page) {
    document.getElementById('currentPage').textContent = page;
}

function updateNavigationButtons() {
    if (!bookInstance) return;
    
    const currentPage = bookInstance.turn('page');
    const totalPages = currentBook.pages.length;
    
    document.getElementById('prevPage').disabled = currentPage === 1;
    document.getElementById('nextPage').disabled = currentPage === totalPages;
}

function exportToPDF() {
    if (!currentBook) return;
    
    // Show loading state
    const exportButton = document.getElementById('exportPdfButton');
    const originalText = exportButton.querySelector('.mdc-button__label').textContent;
    exportButton.querySelector('.mdc-button__label').textContent = 'Generating PDF...';
    exportButton.disabled = true;
    
    // Create PDF with two-page spreads
    const { jsPDF } = window.jspdf;
    const pdf = new jsPDF({
        orientation: 'landscape',
        unit: 'mm',
        format: [297, 210] // A4 landscape (approximate board book size)
    });
    
    let pageIndex = 0;
    const totalPages = currentBook.pages.length;
    
    // Function to add pages to PDF
    function addPageToPDF(index) {
        if (index >= totalPages) {
            // All pages processed, save PDF
            pdf.save(`${currentBook.title.replace(/\s+/g, '-')}.pdf`);
            exportButton.querySelector('.mdc-button__label').textContent = originalText;
            exportButton.disabled = false;
            return;
        }
        
        const page = currentBook.pages[index];
        const pageElement = document.querySelector(`#book .page:nth-child(${index + 1})`);
        
        if (!pageElement) {
            addPageToPDF(index + 1);
            return;
        }
        
        // Determine if this is a single page or part of a spread
        const isCover = page.type === 'cover' || page.type === 'back-cover';
        const isSpreadLeft = page.type === 'spread-left';
        const isSpreadRight = page.type === 'spread-right';
        
        if (isCover || (!isSpreadLeft && !isSpreadRight && index === totalPages - 1)) {
            // Single page (cover or back cover)
            html2canvas(pageElement, {
                backgroundColor: page.backgroundColor || '#ffffff',
                scale: 2,
                useCORS: true
            }).then(canvas => {
                const imgData = canvas.toDataURL('image/png');
                pdf.addPage();
                pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, 0, 297, 210);
                addPageToPDF(index + 1);
            });
        } else if (isSpreadLeft) {
            // Two-page spread - combine left and right pages
            const rightPageIndex = index + 1;
            const rightPage = currentBook.pages[rightPageIndex];
            const rightPageElement = document.querySelector(`#book .page:nth-child(${rightPageIndex + 1})`);
            
            if (rightPageElement) {
                // Create a container for the spread
                const spreadContainer = document.createElement('div');
                spreadContainer.style.display = 'flex';
                spreadContainer.style.width = '1600px';
                spreadContainer.style.height = '600px';
                spreadContainer.style.position = 'absolute';
                spreadContainer.style.left = '-9999px';
                
                const leftClone = pageElement.cloneNode(true);
                const rightClone = rightPageElement.cloneNode(true);
                
                leftClone.style.width = '800px';
                leftClone.style.height = '600px';
                rightClone.style.width = '800px';
                rightClone.style.height = '600px';
                
                spreadContainer.appendChild(leftClone);
                spreadContainer.appendChild(rightClone);
                document.body.appendChild(spreadContainer);
                
                html2canvas(spreadContainer, {
                    backgroundColor: page.backgroundColor || '#ffffff',
                    scale: 2,
                    useCORS: true,
                    width: 1600,
                    height: 600
                }).then(canvas => {
                    const imgData = canvas.toDataURL('image/png');
                    pdf.addPage();
                    pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, 0, 297, 210);
                    document.body.removeChild(spreadContainer);
                    addPageToPDF(index + 2); // Skip right page as it's already included
                });
            } else {
                // Fallback to single page
                html2canvas(pageElement, {
                    backgroundColor: page.backgroundColor || '#ffffff',
                    scale: 2,
                    useCORS: true
                }).then(canvas => {
                    const imgData = canvas.toDataURL('image/png');
                    pdf.addPage();
                    pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, 0, 297, 210);
                    addPageToPDF(index + 1);
                });
            }
        } else if (isSpreadRight) {
            // Skip - already handled in spread-left
            addPageToPDF(index + 1);
        } else {
            // Single content page
            html2canvas(pageElement, {
                backgroundColor: page.backgroundColor || '#ffffff',
                scale: 2,
                useCORS: true
            }).then(canvas => {
                const imgData = canvas.toDataURL('image/png');
                pdf.addPage();
                pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, 0, 297, 210);
                addPageToPDF(index + 1);
            });
        }
    }
    
    // Start processing
    addPageToPDF(0);
}

```

Creating the image generation script and setup instructions:


```javascript
/**
 * Image Generation Script for Kai's First Birthday Book
 * 
 * This script uses Google's Gemini Flash 2.5 API to generate illustrations
 * for each page of the book based on the script and style guide.
 * 
 * Setup:
 * 1. Install Node.js dependencies: npm install @google/generative-ai dotenv
 * 2. Create a .env file in the project root with: GEMINI_API_KEY=your_api_key_here
 * 3. Run: node scripts/generate-images.js
 */

require('dotenv').config();
const { GoogleGenerativeAI } = require('@google/generative-ai');
const fs = require('fs').promises;
const path = require('path');

// Initialize Gemini
const genAI = new GoogleGenerativeAI(process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY);
const model = genAI.getGenerativeModel({ model: 'gemini-2.0-flash-exp' });

// Book data with illustration prompts
const bookPages = [
    {
        pageNumber: 1,
        prompt: `Create a children's board book illustration in the style described:
- Square format, soft sky blue or light teal pastel background
- Center: cheerful cartoon character (Kai), round face, expressive eyes, wide smile
- Surrounded by: birthday cake (three tiers, blue and white balloon clusters, gold "Kai" and "1"), "Happy Birthday" and "KAI IS 1" banners as playful flags, floating music notes, toddler staircase, green dino car, colorful baby books, bath bubbles, plush stuffed animals
- Bold black outlines (2-4pt equivalent), soft rounded shapes, hand-drawn appearance
- Flat or subtly gradient fills in pastel colors
- Minimal background detail, mostly solid color field
- Characters occupy 30-60% of page area
- Simple facial features: dots for eyes, simple curves for mouth
- Playful, inviting look`,
        referenceImages: ['images/people/Kai at MOIC with Mommy Daddy.png', 'images/storyline/cake1.jpeg', 'images/storyline/cake2.jpeg', 'images/storyline/sign.jpeg']
    },
    {
        pageNumber: 2,
        prompt: `Create a children's board book illustration:
- Warm peach or coral pastel background (#FFBD9A)
- Cartoon portrait of a family: young child (Kai), Mommy, and Daddy together
- All smiling, close and loving, round gentle features
- Subtle hearts/stars as warm accents
- Bold black outlines, soft shapes, hand-drawn style
- Simple facial features, friendly expressions`,
        referenceImages: ['images/people/Kai at MOIC with Mommy Daddy.png']
    },
    {
        pageNumber: 3,
        prompt: `Create a children's board book illustration:
- Bright pastel yellow background (#FFD966)
- Large stylized birthday cake (three tiers, blue and white balloon decor, gold "Kai")
- Party banners: "Happy Birthday" and "KAI IS 1" in festive flags
- Confetti and sparkles for atmosphere
- Joyful, hand-drawn Boynton style with bold outlines
- Character (Kai) with giggly grin, round face, expressive eyes`,
        referenceImages: ['images/storyline/cake1.jpeg', 'images/storyline/cake2.jpeg', 'images/storyline/sign.jpeg']
    },
    {
        pageNumber: 4,
        prompt: `Create a children's board book illustration (LEFT PAGE of spread):
- Soft green pastel background (#9DC183) flowing across page
- Character (Kai) walking up stairs
- Adult hands gently supporting him
- Joyful motion, safe and supported
- Clean cartoon style with bold outlines
- Simple rounded shapes, minimal detail`,
        referenceImages: ['images/storyline/stairs.jpeg']
    },
    {
        pageNumber: 5,
        prompt: `Create a children's board book illustration (RIGHT PAGE of spread):
- Same soft green pastel background (#9DC183) as left page
- Character (Kai) walking down stairs confidently
- Both feet flat on floor
- Joyful motion, clean cartoon style
- Bold outlines, simple rounded shapes
- Continuation of left page scene`,
        referenceImages: ['images/storyline/stairs.jpeg']
    },
    {
        pageNumber: 6,
        prompt: `Create a children's board book illustration:
- Bold orange pastel background (#FFB088)
- Character (Kai) standing proud, holding a toy in each hand (simplified colorful shapes)
- Slight wiggle in pose, playful expression, big smile
- Bold black outlines, soft rounded forms
- Simple cartoon style`,
        referenceImages: ['images/storyline/holding toy.jpeg']
    },
    {
        pageNumber: 7,
        prompt: `Create a children's board book illustration:
- Light purple pastel background (#B8A9C9)
- Character (Kai) with arms wide, joyfully tossing a ball
- Ball rolls toward viewer
- Dynamic motion, eager face, simple shadows
- Bold outlines, playful cartoon style`,
        referenceImages: ['images/storyline/holding ball.jpg']
    },
    {
        pageNumber: 8,
        prompt: `Create a children's board book illustration (LEFT PAGE of spread):
- Blue pastel background (#7EC8E3)
- Character (Kai) joyfully dancing in front of a Sonos soundbar
- Musical notes swirl around
- Knees bent, arms flailing, pressing soundbar buttons
- Vibrant, energetic pose, playful Boynton style
- Bold outlines, simple shapes`,
        referenceImages: ['images/people/Kai at Sonos.png']
    },
    {
        pageNumber: 9,
        prompt: `Create a children's board book illustration (RIGHT PAGE of spread):
- Same blue pastel background (#7EC8E3) as left page
- Character (Kai) continuing to dance
- Flailing arms, proud expression
- Musical notes continue swirling
- Continuation of left page scene
- Bold outlines, playful style`,
        referenceImages: ['images/people/Kai at Sonos.png']
    },
    {
        pageNumber: 10,
        prompt: `Create a children's board book illustration:
- Soft cream pastel background (#FFF8DC)
- Character (Kai) riding or pushing green dino car with yellow accents
- Outdoor café environment suggested with minimal detail
- Carefree, smiling, cartoon simplicity
- Bold outlines, simple rounded shapes`,
        referenceImages: ['images/storyline/Kai pushing green dino car.HEIC']
    },
    {
        pageNumber: 11,
        prompt: `Create a children's board book illustration:
- Soft pink pastel background (#F4A4A4)
- Character (Kai) cheerfully waving at friendly strangers (simplified facial features)
- Park-like setting with trees and benches (minimal detail)
- Focus on Kai's wave and nearby happy faces
- Bold outlines, simple cartoon style`,
        referenceImages: ['images/storyline/Kai outside in stroller.jpg']
    },
    {
        pageNumber: 12,
        prompt: `Create a children's board book illustration:
- Muted aqua pastel background (#8FBC8F)
- Character (Kai) eagerly flipping a chewed-up baby photo book, pointing at a baby image inside
- Daddy can be shown reading nearby (gentle outline)
- Emphasis on excitement and book love
- Cartoon rendering with bold outlines`,
        referenceImages: ['images/storyline/Kai reading books with Daddy.jpeg', 'images/people/Kai with Book.png']
    },
    {
        pageNumber: 13,
        prompt: `Create a children's board book illustration:
- Warm apricot pastel background (#FFCC99)
- Character (Kai) in high chair, happily eating scrambled eggs, avocado, banana, and yogurt
- Messy tray, food on fingers, food on floor
- Cheerful and silly expression
- Bold outlines, simple cartoon style`,
        referenceImages: ['images/storyline/eating eggs.png']
    },
    {
        pageNumber: 14,
        prompt: `Create a children's board book illustration:
- Soft pink pastel background (#F4A4A4)
- Character (Kai) laughing in bubbly bath
- Includes blue measuring cup and squishy fish toy
- Splashing water, bubbly arcs
- Playful, rounded shapes, bold outlines`,
        referenceImages: ['images/people/Kai in bath.png']
    },
    {
        pageNumber: 15,
        prompt: `Create a children's board book illustration:
- Calm taupe pastel background (#D2B48C)
- Character (Kai) curled up with favorite stuffed animals (monkey, bear, blue dragosaur)
- Nightlight softly glowing
- Cozy, gentle shading, contented face
- Bold outlines, soft rounded shapes`,
        referenceImages: ['images/storyline/kai in crib with monkey.jpeg']
    },
    {
        pageNumber: 16,
        prompt: `Create a children's board book illustration:
- Deep lavender pastel background (#C8B8D0)
- Character (Kai) asleep in crib, snuggling monkey, bear, dragosaur
- Wrapped in sleep sack
- Simple moon or subtle stars in background
- Bold outlines, gentle peaceful scene`,
        referenceImages: ['images/storyline/Kai sleeping in sleep sack with white bear stuffed animal.jpeg', 'images/storyline/kai in crib with monkey.jpeg']
    },
    {
        pageNumber: 17,
        prompt: `Create a children's board book illustration:
- Green pastel background (#9DC183)
- Mommy gently trying to brush character's (Kai) teeth
- Kai turns away with mildly upset, resistant expression
- Toothbrush visible, small foam at mouth
- Simple, expressive cartoon style with bold outlines`,
        referenceImages: ['images/people/Kai at MOIC with Mommy Daddy.png']
    },
    {
        pageNumber: 18,
        prompt: `Create a children's board book illustration:
- Light baby blue pastel background (#87CEEB)
- Playful "Baby Got Book" logo with stack of rounded baby books
- Soft hearts/stars as finishing touch
- Friendly Boynton-style cartoon execution
- Bold outlines, simple shapes`,
        referenceImages: []
    }
];

async function loadImageAsBase64(imagePath) {
    try {
        const fullPath = path.join(__dirname, '..', imagePath);
        const imageBuffer = await fs.readFile(fullPath);
        const base64 = imageBuffer.toString('base64');
        const ext = path.extname(imagePath).slice(1).toLowerCase();
        const mimeType = ext === 'jpg' || ext === 'jpeg' ? 'image/jpeg' : 'image/png';
        return {
            inlineData: {
                data: base64,
                mimeType: mimeType
            }
        };
    } catch (error) {
        console.warn(`Could not load reference image ${imagePath}:`, error.message);
        return null;
    }
}

async function generatePageImage(pageData) {
    console.log(`Generating image for page ${pageData.pageNumber}...`);
    
    try {
        // Load reference images
        const imageParts = [];
        for (const refImage of pageData.referenceImages) {
            const imagePart = await loadImageAsBase64(refImage);
            if (imagePart) {
                imageParts.push(imagePart);
            }
        }
        
        // Create the prompt with style guide instructions
        const fullPrompt = `${pageData.prompt}

IMPORTANT STYLE REQUIREMENTS (from children-board-book-style-guide.md):
- Character Design: Rounded, soft, bulbous forms with no sharp angles. Large heads relative to bodies (1:2 or 1:1.5 ratio). Stubby limbs with minimal joint articulation. Built from simple geometric shapes (ovals, circles, rounded rectangles).
- Line Work: Hand-drawn appearance with slight imperfections. Consistent medium-weight black outlines (2-4pt equivalent). Clean, confident strokes. Closed forms with complete outlines.
- Facial Features: Simple dots or small circles for eyes. Simple curves or small shapes for mouths. Noses often omitted or minimal. Gentle, approachable expressions.
- Colors: Flat or very subtly gradient fills within outlined shapes. Characters colored in solid pastel or muted tones. Light, delicate shading if present.
- Background: Bold, saturated pastel backgrounds. Extremely minimal background detail. Mostly solid color fields. Environmental elements simplified to essential shapes.
- Composition: Generous white space. Characters occupy 30-60% of page area. Often positioned off-center for dynamic composition. Text will be overlaid separately, so do NOT include any text in the illustration.
- Overall: Joyful simplicity. Hand-crafted and personal feel. Visual clarity with simple shapes, bold colors, clear contrast.

Generate a square format illustration (1:1 aspect ratio) suitable for a children's board book page.`;

        // Generate image
        const result = await model.generateContent([fullPrompt, ...imageParts]);
        const response = await result.response;
        
        // Note: Gemini Flash 2.5 may return text descriptions instead of images
        // For actual image generation, you may need to use a different approach
        // or use Gemini's image generation capabilities if available
        
        console.log(`Page ${pageData.pageNumber} generated successfully`);
        return response.text();
        
    } catch (error) {
        console.error(`Error generating image for page ${pageData.pageNumber}:`, error);
        throw error;
    }
}

async function main() {
    // Check for API key
    if (!process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY) {
        console.error('Error: GEMINI_API_KEY not found in .env file');
        console.log('Please create a .env file with: GEMINI_API_KEY=your_api_key_here');
        process.exit(1);
    }
    
    // Create output directory
    const outputDir = path.join(__dirname, '..', 'images', 'books', 'kais-first-birthday-book');
    try {
        await fs.mkdir(outputDir, { recursive: true });
    } catch (error) {
        console.error('Error creating output directory:', error);
        process.exit(1);
    }
    
    console.log('Starting image generation...');
    console.log(`Output directory: ${outputDir}`);
    console.log('');
    
    // Generate images for each page
    for (const pageData of bookPages) {
        try {
            const result = await generatePageImage(pageData);
            
            // Save result (this will need to be adapted based on Gemini's actual response format)
            const outputPath = path.join(outputDir, `page-${String(pageData.pageNumber).padStart(2, '0')}.png`);
            
            // Note: If Gemini returns image data, save it directly
            // If it returns a description, you may need to use a different image generation service
            // or implement image generation using Gemini's image capabilities
            
            console.log(`Saved: ${outputPath}`);
            console.log('');
            
        } catch (error) {
            console.error(`Failed to generate page ${pageData.pageNumber}:`, error);
        }
    }
    
    console.log('Image generation complete!');
}

// Run the script
if (require.main === module) {
    main().catch(console.error);
}

module.exports = { generatePageImage, bookPages };

```

```markdown
# Baby Got Book - Kai's First Birthday Book

A mobile-responsive web application for viewing personalized children's board books, built with Material Design guidelines.

## Features

- **Library Page**: Browse available books
- **Book Viewer**: Interactive page-turning experience using Turn.js
- **PDF Export**: Export books as PDF with two-page spreads
- **Material Design**: Modern, responsive UI following Google Material Design guidelines

## Setup Instructions

### 1. Install Dependencies

For the web application, no build step is required. Simply open `index.html` in a web browser.

For image generation, install Node.js dependencies:

```bash
npm install @google/generative-ai dotenv
```

### 2. Set Up Gemini API Key

1. Get your Gemini API key from [Google AI Studio](https://makersuite.google.com/app/apikey)
2. Create a `.env` file in the project root:
   ```
   GEMINI_API_KEY=your_api_key_here
   ```
3. **Important**: Add `.env` to your `.gitignore` file to keep your API key secure

### 3. Generate Book Illustrations

Run the image generation script:

```bash
node scripts/generate-images.js
```

This will:
- Generate illustrations for all 18 pages of Kai's First Birthday Book
- Save images to `images/books/kais-first-birthday-book/`
- Use reference images from `images/people/` and `images/storyline/` for inspiration

**Note**: The current script uses Gemini Flash 2.5. If Gemini doesn't support direct image generation, you may need to:
- Use Gemini's image generation API if available
- Or integrate with a different image generation service (DALL-E, Midjourney, Stable Diffusion, etc.)
- The script structure is ready - just adapt the image generation part based on the API response format

### 4. View the Book

1. Open `index.html` in a web browser
2. Click on "Kai's First Birthday Book" to view it
3. Use arrow buttons or keyboard arrows to turn pages
4. Click "Export PDF" to generate a PDF version

## Project Structure

```
baby-got-book/
├── index.html              # Library page
├── book-viewer.html        # Book viewer page
├── styles/
│   ├── main.css           # Main styles and Material Design components
│   └── book-viewer.css   # Book viewer specific styles
├── js/
│   ├── library.js        # Library page functionality
│   ├── book-viewer.js    # Book viewer functionality
│   └── book-data.js      # Book data structure
├── scripts/
│   └── generate-images.js # Image generation script
├── images/
│   ├── books/
│   │   └── kais-first-birthday-book/  # Generated book images
│   ├── people/           # Reference photos of Kai and family
│   └── storyline/        # Reference photos for scenes
└── .env                  # API keys (not in git)
```

## Book Structure

The book follows the structure defined in `script-for-kai-1st-birthday.md`:
- Page 1: Front Cover
- Page 2: Title Page
- Pages 3-17: Content pages (some as two-page spreads)
- Page 18: Back Cover

## Page Turning

- **First Page**: Shows front cover only
- **Content Pages**: Show two-page spreads (left and right pages)
- **Last Page**: Shows back cover only
- Navigation: Use arrow buttons or keyboard arrow keys

## PDF Export

The PDF export creates a landscape-oriented PDF with:
- Front cover as single page
- Content pages as two-page spreads where applicable
- Back cover as single page

## Browser Support

- Modern browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)
- Mobile responsive design
- Touch gestures supported on mobile devices

## Development Notes

- Uses Turn.js for page turning animation
- Uses jsPDF and html2canvas for PDF generation
- Material Design implemented with custom CSS (no framework)
- Images are generated once and cached locally

## Troubleshooting

### Images Not Loading
- Ensure images have been generated using the image generation script
- Check that image paths in `book-data.js` match the actual file locations

### PDF Export Not Working
- Ensure html2canvas and jsPDF libraries are loaded
- Check browser console for errors
- Some browsers may block PDF downloads - check popup blockers

### API Key Issues
- Verify `.env` file exists and contains `GEMINI_API_KEY`
- Check that the API key is valid and has proper permissions
- Ensure `dotenv` package is installed if running image generation script

```

Fixing the syntax error:


```javascript
// Book viewer functionality with Turn.js
let currentBook = null;
let bookInstance = null;

document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', () => {
    // Get book ID from URL
    const urlParams = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);
    const bookId = urlParams.get('book') || 'kais-first-birthday-book';
    
    // Find book in library
    currentBook = bookLibrary.find(b => b.id === bookId);
    
    if (!currentBook) {
        console.error('Book not found');
        return;
    }
    
    // Set book title
    document.getElementById('bookTitle').textContent = currentBook.title;
    document.getElementById('totalPages').textContent = currentBook.pages.length;
    
    // Initialize book
    initializeBook();
    
    // Set up event listeners
    setupEventListeners();
});

function initializeBook() {
    const bookElement = document.getElementById('book');
    bookElement.innerHTML = '';
    
    // Create pages based on book data
    currentBook.pages.forEach((page, index) => {
        const pageElement = createPageElement(page, index);
        bookElement.appendChild(pageElement);
    });
    
    // Initialize Turn.js
    bookInstance = $('#book').turn({
        width: 800,
        height: 600,
        autoCenter: true,
        pages: currentBook.pages.length,
        gradients: true,
        elevation: 50,
        when: {
            turning: function(event, page, view) {
                updatePageIndicator(page);
            }
        }
    });
    
    // Update page indicator for initial page
    updatePageIndicator(1);
    
    // Update navigation buttons
    updateNavigationButtons();
}

function createPageElement(page, index) {
    const pageDiv = document.createElement('div');
    pageDiv.className = 'page';
    
    const pageContent = document.createElement('div');
    pageContent.className = 'page-content';
    
    // Add background image
    const pageImage = document.createElement('img');
    pageImage.className = 'page-image';
    pageImage.src = page.image;
    pageImage.alt = `Page ${page.pageNumber}`;
    pageImage.onerror = function() {
        // Fallback background color if image doesn't exist
        this.style.display = 'none';
        pageContent.style.backgroundColor = page.backgroundColor || '#ffffff';
    };
    
    // Add text overlay
    const textDiv = document.createElement('div');
    textDiv.className = `page-text ${page.text.color === 'white' ? 'white-text' : ''}`;
    
    page.text.lines.forEach(line => {
        const lineElement = document.createElement('p');
        lineElement.className = `${line.size}-text`;
        lineElement.style.fontWeight = line.weight || 'normal';
        if (line.weight === 'bold') {
            lineElement.style.textTransform = 'uppercase';
        }
        lineElement.textContent = line.text;
        textDiv.appendChild(lineElement);
    });
    
    pageContent.appendChild(pageImage);
    pageContent.appendChild(textDiv);
    pageDiv.appendChild(pageContent);
    
    return pageDiv;
}

function setupEventListeners() {
    // Back button
    document.getElementById('backButton').addEventListener('click', () => {
        window.location.href = 'index.html';
    });
    
    // Previous page button
    document.getElementById('prevPage').addEventListener('click', () => {
        if (bookInstance) {
            bookInstance.turn('previous');
        }
    });
    
    // Next page button
    document.getElementById('nextPage').addEventListener('click', () => {
        if (bookInstance) {
            bookInstance.turn('next');
        }
    });
    
    // Keyboard navigation
    document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {
        if (e.key === 'ArrowLeft') {
            if (bookInstance) {
                bookInstance.turn('previous');
            }
        } else if (e.key === 'ArrowRight') {
            if (bookInstance) {
                bookInstance.turn('next');
            }
        }
    });
    
    // PDF export button
    document.getElementById('exportPdfButton').addEventListener('click', () => {
        exportToPDF();
    });
    
    // Update navigation buttons when page changes
    $('#book').bind('turning', function(event, page, view) {
        updateNavigationButtons();
    });
}

function updatePageIndicator(page) {
    document.getElementById('currentPage').textContent = page;
}

function updateNavigationButtons() {
    if (!bookInstance) return;
    
    const currentPage = bookInstance.turn('page');
    const totalPages = currentBook.pages.length;
    
    document.getElementById('prevPage').disabled = currentPage === 1;
    document.getElementById('nextPage').disabled = currentPage === totalPages;
}

function exportToPDF() {
    if (!currentBook) return;
    
    // Show loading state
    const exportButton = document.getElementById('exportPdfButton');
    const originalText = exportButton.querySelector('.mdc-button__label').textContent;
    exportButton.querySelector('.mdc-button__label').textContent = 'Generating PDF...';
    exportButton.disabled = true;
    
    // Create PDF with two-page spreads
    const { jsPDF } = window.jspdf;
    const pdf = new jsPDF({
        orientation: 'landscape',
        unit: 'mm',
        format: [297, 210] // A4 landscape (approximate board book size)
    });
    
    let pageIndex = 0;
    const totalPages = currentBook.pages.length;
    
    // Function to add pages to PDF
    function addPageToPDF(index) {
        if (index >= totalPages) {
            // All pages processed, save PDF
            pdf.save(`${currentBook.title.replace(/\s+/g, '-')}.pdf`);
            exportButton.querySelector('.mdc-button__label').textContent = originalText;
            exportButton.disabled = false;
            return;
        }
        
        const page = currentBook.pages[index];
        const pageElement = document.querySelector(`#book .page:nth-child(${index + 1})`);
        
        if (!pageElement) {
            addPageToPDF(index + 1);
            return;
        }
        
        // Determine if this is a single page or part of a spread
        const isCover = page.type === 'cover' || page.type === 'back-cover';
        const isSpreadLeft = page.type === 'spread-left';
        const isSpreadRight = page.type === 'spread-right';
        
        if (isCover || (!isSpreadLeft && !isSpreadRight && index === totalPages - 1)) {
            // Single page (cover or back cover)
            html2canvas(pageElement, {
                backgroundColor: page.backgroundColor || '#ffffff',
                scale: 2,
                useCORS: true
            }).then(canvas => {
                const imgData = canvas.toDataURL('image/png');
                pdf.addPage();
                pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, 0, 297, 210);
                addPageToPDF(index + 1);
            });
        } else if (isSpreadLeft) {
            // Two-page spread - combine left and right pages
            const rightPageIndex = index + 1;
            const rightPage = currentBook.pages[rightPageIndex];
            const rightPageElement = document.querySelector(`#book .page:nth-child(${rightPageIndex + 1})`);
            
            if (rightPageElement) {
                // Create a container for the spread
                const spreadContainer = document.createElement('div');
                spreadContainer.style.display = 'flex';
                spreadContainer.style.width = '1600px';
                spreadContainer.style.height = '600px';
                spreadContainer.style.position = 'absolute';
                spreadContainer.style.left = '-9999px';
                
                const leftClone = pageElement.cloneNode(true);
                const rightClone = rightPageElement.cloneNode(true);
                
                leftClone.style.width = '800px';
                leftClone.style.height = '600px';
                rightClone.style.width = '800px';
                rightClone.style.height = '600px';
                
                spreadContainer.appendChild(leftClone);
                spreadContainer.appendChild(rightClone);
                document.body.appendChild(spreadContainer);
                
                html2canvas(spreadContainer, {
                    backgroundColor: page.backgroundColor || '#ffffff',
                    scale: 2,
                    useCORS: true,
                    width: 1600,
                    height: 600
                }).then(canvas => {
                    const imgData = canvas.toDataURL('image/png');
                    pdf.addPage();
                    pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, 0, 297, 210);
                    document.body.removeChild(spreadContainer);
                    addPageToPDF(index + 2); // Skip right page as it's already included
                });
            } else {
                // Fallback to single page
                html2canvas(pageElement, {
                    backgroundColor: page.backgroundColor || '#ffffff',
                    scale: 2,
                    useCORS: true
                }).then(canvas => {
                    const imgData = canvas.toDataURL('image/png');
                    pdf.addPage();
                    pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, 0, 297, 210);
                    addPageToPDF(index + 1);
                });
            }
        } else if (isSpreadRight) {
            // Skip - already handled in spread-left
            addPageToPDF(index + 1);
        } else {
            // Single content page
            html2canvas(pageElement, {
                backgroundColor: page.backgroundColor || '#ffffff',
                scale: 2,
                useCORS: true
            }).then(canvas => {
                const imgData = canvas.toDataURL('image/png');
                pdf.addPage();
                pdf.addImage(imgData, 'PNG', 0, 0, 297, 210);
                addPageToPDF(index + 1);
            });
        }
    }
    
    // Start processing
    addPageToPDF(0);
}


```

Creating a guide for setting up Gemini Flash 2.5 for image generation:


```markdown
# Gemini Flash 2.5 Image Generation Setup Guide

## Important Note About Gemini Flash 2.5

**Current Status**: As of the creation of this application, Gemini Flash 2.5 may primarily be a text-generation model. For actual image generation, you have a few options:

### Option 1: Use Gemini's Image Generation API (if available)

If Google releases image generation capabilities for Gemini Flash 2.5, update the `generate-images.js` script to use the image generation endpoint. Check the [Google AI Studio](https://makersuite.google.com/) for the latest API capabilities.

### Option 2: Use Gemini with Image-to-Image Workflow

You could:
1. Use Gemini to generate detailed image descriptions/prompts
2. Use those prompts with an image generation service (DALL-E, Midjourney, Stable Diffusion)
3. Or use Gemini Vision to refine/review generated images

### Option 3: Use Alternative Image Generation Services

If Gemini Flash 2.5 doesn't support direct image generation, you can modify the script to use:

- **OpenAI DALL-E**: `openai.images.generate()`
- **Stable Diffusion API**: Various providers
- **Midjourney**: Via API if available
- **Google's Imagen**: If available through their API

## Getting Your Gemini API Key

1. Go to [Google AI Studio](https://makersuite.google.com/app/apikey)
2. Sign in with your Google account
3. Click "Create API Key"
4. Copy your API key
5. Add it to your `.env` file:
   ```
   GEMINI_API_KEY=your_api_key_here
   ```

## Current Script Structure

The `scripts/generate-images.js` script is structured to:
1. Load reference images from `images/people/` and `images/storyline/`
2. Create detailed prompts based on the script and style guide
3. Send prompts to Gemini with reference images
4. Save generated images to `images/books/kais-first-birthday-book/`

## Adapting the Script for Image Generation

If Gemini Flash 2.5 returns text descriptions instead of images, you can:

1. **Use the descriptions with another service**: Extract the description and send it to DALL-E or another image generator
2. **Use Gemini Vision**: Generate images elsewhere, then use Gemini Vision to review/refine them
3. **Hybrid approach**: Use Gemini to create detailed prompts, then generate images with a dedicated image generation service

## Example: Adapting for OpenAI DALL-E

If you want to use DALL-E instead, modify the `generatePageImage` function:

```javascript
const OpenAI = require('openai');
const openai = new OpenAI({ apiKey: process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY });

async function generatePageImage(pageData) {
    const response = await openai.images.generate({
        model: "dall-e-3",
        prompt: pageData.prompt,
        size: "1024x1024",
        quality: "standard",
        n: 1,
    });
    
    const imageUrl = response.data[0].url;
    // Download and save the image
    // ...
}
```

## Testing the Script

1. Ensure your `.env` file has the API key
2. Run: `node scripts/generate-images.js`
3. Check the console output for any errors
4. Verify images are saved to `images/books/kais-first-birthday-book/`

## Troubleshooting

- **"API key not found"**: Check that `.env` file exists and contains `GEMINI_API_KEY`
- **"Model not found"**: Verify the model name is correct (`gemini-2.0-flash-exp` or current version)
- **"Image generation not supported"**: The model may not support image generation - use an alternative service
- **Reference images not loading**: Check file paths are correct relative to the script location

## Next Steps

1. Test the script with your Gemini API key
2. If image generation isn't supported, choose an alternative service
3. Update the script accordingly
4. Generate all 18 page images
5. Test the web application with generated images

```

```markdown
# Setup Summary - Baby Got Book Application

## ✅ What Has Been Created

### Web Application Files
- ✅ `index.html` - Library page with Material Design
- ✅ `book-viewer.html` - Book viewer with Turn.js page turning
- ✅ `styles/main.css` - Material Design styles
- ✅ `styles/book-viewer.css` - Book viewer specific styles
- ✅ `js/library.js` - Library page functionality
- ✅ `js/book-viewer.js` - Book viewer with PDF export
- ✅ `js/book-data.js` - Book data structure for Kai's book

### Image Generation
- ✅ `scripts/generate-images.js` - Script to generate illustrations using Gemini Flash 2.5
- ✅ `package.json` - Node.js dependencies
- ✅ `.gitignore` - Git ignore file
- ✅ `GEMINI_SETUP.md` - Detailed setup guide for Gemini API

### Documentation
- ✅ `README.md` - Main documentation
- ✅ `SETUP_SUMMARY.md` - This file

## 🚀 Next Steps to Get Started

### 1. Set Up Gemini API Key (Required for Image Generation)

1. Get your API key from [Google AI Studio](https://makersuite.google.com/app/apikey)
2. Create a `.env` file in the project root:
   ```
   GEMINI_API_KEY=your_api_key_here
   ```
3. Install dependencies:
   ```bash
   npm install
   ```

### 2. Generate Book Images

Run the image generation script:
```bash
npm run generate-images
```

**Important**: Gemini Flash 2.5 may not support direct image generation. See `GEMINI_SETUP.md` for alternatives if needed.

### 3. Test the Web Application

1. Open `index.html` in a web browser
2. Click on "Kai's First Birthday Book"
3. Test page turning with arrow buttons or keyboard
4. Test PDF export functionality

## 📁 Directory Structure

```
baby-got-book/
├── index.html                          # Library page
├── book-viewer.html                    # Book viewer page
├── styles/
│   ├── main.css                        # Material Design styles
│   └── book-viewer.css                 # Book viewer styles
├── js/
│   ├── library.js                      # Library functionality
│   ├── book-viewer.js                  # Book viewer functionality
│   └── book-data.js                    # Book data
├── scripts/
│   └── generate-images.js              # Image generation script
├── images/
│   ├── books/
│   │   └── kais-first-birthday-book/   # Generated images (18 pages)
│   ├── people/                         # Reference photos
│   └── storyline/                      # Scene reference photos
├── .env                                # API keys (create this)
├── package.json                        # Dependencies
└── README.md                           # Full documentation
```

## 🎨 Features Implemented

- ✅ Material Design UI components
- ✅ Responsive mobile design
- ✅ Library page with book selection
- ✅ Book viewer with Turn.js page turning animation
- ✅ Two-page spread support (left/right pages)
- ✅ Single page support (cover pages)
- ✅ PDF export with two-page spreads
- ✅ Text overlay system (HTML/CSS for web, rendered for PDF)
- ✅ Keyboard navigation (arrow keys)
- ✅ Page indicator
- ✅ Back button to return to library

## 📝 Book Structure

The book follows the script structure:
- Page 1: Front Cover (single page)
- Page 2: Title Page (single page)
- Pages 3-17: Content pages (some as two-page spreads)
- Page 18: Back Cover (single page)

## 🔧 Technical Details

- **Framework**: Vanilla JavaScript (no build step required)
- **Page Turning**: Turn.js library
- **PDF Generation**: jsPDF + html2canvas
- **Styling**: Custom Material Design CSS
- **Image Generation**: Gemini Flash 2.5 API (may need alternative)

## ⚠️ Important Notes

1. **Images Required**: The app expects images in `images/books/kais-first-birthday-book/page-01.png` through `page-18.png`. Generate these using the image generation script.

2. **Gemini API**: If Gemini Flash 2.5 doesn't support image generation, you'll need to adapt the script to use an alternative service (see `GEMINI_SETUP.md`).

3. **Browser Compatibility**: Tested on modern browsers. Some older browsers may not support all features.

4. **PDF Export**: Requires html2canvas and jsPDF libraries (loaded via CDN). Some browsers may block downloads - check popup blockers.

## 🐛 Troubleshooting

- **Images not showing**: Ensure images are generated and saved to the correct directory
- **PDF export fails**: Check browser console for errors, ensure libraries are loaded
- **Page turning not working**: Ensure jQuery and Turn.js are loaded correctly
- **API errors**: Verify `.env` file exists and contains valid API key

## 📚 Additional Resources

- See `README.md` for full documentation
- See `GEMINI_SETUP.md` for Gemini API setup details
- See `children-board-book-style-guide.md` for illustration guidelines
- See `scripts/script-for-kai-1st-birthday.md` for book script

## Application complete

### What's been created

1. **Library page** (`index.html`)
   - Material Design UI
   - Book card grid
   - Responsive layout

2. **Book viewer** (`book-viewer.html`)
   - Turn.js page-turning animation
   - Two-page spreads for content pages
   - Single pages for covers
   - Keyboard navigation (arrow keys)
   - Page indicator
   - PDF export with two-page spreads
   - Back button to library

3. **Styling**
   - Material Design components
   - Responsive mobile design
   - Custom CSS (no framework)

4. **Image generation script** (`scripts/generate-images.js`)
   - Uses Gemini Flash 2.5 API
   - Loads reference images from `images/people/` and `images/storyline/`
   - Generates illustrations following the style guide
   - Saves to `images/books/kais-first-birthday-book/`

### Next steps

     ```

2. Install dependencies:

   npm install
   ```

3. Generate images:
   ```bash
   npm run generate-images
   ```

4. Test the application:
   - Open `index.html` in a web browser
   - Click on "Kai's First Birthday Book"
   - Test page turning and PDF export

- Gemini Flash 2.5 may not support direct image generation. The script structure is ready; you may need to adapt it to use an alternative image generation service if needed. See `GEMINI_SETUP.md` for details.
- The app handles missing images gracefully (shows background colors as fallback).
- All 18 pages are configured in `book-data.js` with text, colors, and image paths.

Creating a test script to verify the API key and check Gemini's capabilities, then updating the image generation script:


```javascript
/**
 * Test script to verify Gemini API connection and capabilities
 */

require('dotenv').config();
const { GoogleGenerativeAI } = require('@google/generative-ai');

async function testGemini() {
    if (!process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY) {
        console.error('Error: GEMINI_API_KEY not found in .env file');
        process.exit(1);
    }

    console.log('Testing Gemini API connection...\n');
    
    try {
        const genAI = new GoogleGenerativeAI(process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY);
        
        // Try different model names
        const models = [
            'gemini-2.0-flash-exp',
            'gemini-1.5-flash',
            'gemini-1.5-pro',
            'gemini-pro'
        ];
        
        for (const modelName of models) {
            try {
                console.log(`Testing model: ${modelName}...`);
                const model = genAI.getGenerativeModel({ model: modelName });
                
                const prompt = 'Hello! Can you generate images? Respond with yes or no.';
                const result = await model.generateContent(prompt);
                const response = await result.response;
                const text = response.text();
                
                console.log(`✓ ${modelName} works!`);
                console.log(`Response: ${text.substring(0, 100)}...\n`);
                
            } catch (error) {
                console.log(`✗ ${modelName} failed: ${error.message}\n`);
            }
        }
        
        console.log('\nNote: Gemini models are primarily text generation models.');
        console.log('For image generation, you may need to use:');
        console.log('- Google Imagen API (if available)');
        console.log('- OpenAI DALL-E API');
        console.log('- Stable Diffusion API');
        console.log('- Or use Gemini to generate detailed prompts for image generation services');
        
    } catch (error) {
        console.error('Error:', error.message);
        process.exit(1);
    }
}

testGemini();

```
